S.FORM: == kawayna == ťáĎťäÄŤäĪ taneng miazih atu mukan. sikapahay a sangek i labii. u masay-kilang a nipaluma'. malicangay a balu taneng micebus tu akuti'ay a nanum, taneng mukan. kagak kakuniza subal :Áúüś†łŚüü Eukaryota salaedan :ś§ćÁČ©ÁēĆ Plantae panan :ŤĘęŚ≠źś§ćÁČ©ťĖÄ Magnoliophyta upiz :ťõôŚ≠źŤĎČś§ćÁČ©Á∂Ī Magnoliopsida mata :Ś∑ĚÁļĆśĖ∑Áõģ Dipsacales sapamat :ŚŅćŚÜ¨ÁßĎ Caprifoliaceae mikitinay :ŚŅćŚÜ¨ŚĪ¨ Lonicera hicahicaay :ŚŅćŚÜ¨ L. japonica u siwkay nu nipaluma u sanek nu nipaluma malaheci tu imelang pasubana i cacudadan a lacul u zuma a cidekay a kamu, saungay misapayu' a cidekay u sulit nu zuma a kamu-Hulam atu Amilika ŚŅćŚÜ¨ , . Lonicera japonica, known as golden-and-silver honeysuckle or Japanese honeysuckle in English, suikazura in Japanese, jinyinhua or rendongteng in Chinese, indongdeonggul in Korean, and kim ng√Ęn hoa in Vietnamese, is a species of honeysuckle native to eastern Asia including China, Japan and Korea. It is a twining vine able to climb up to 10 m (33 ft) high or more in trees, with opposite, simple oval leaves 3‚Äď8 cm (1.2‚Äď3.1 in) long and 2‚Äď3 cm (0.79‚Äď1.18 in) broad. The flowers are double-tongued, opening white and fading to yellow, and sweetly vanilla scented. The fruit is a black spherical berry 3‚Äď4 mm (0.12‚Äď0.16 in) diameter containing a few seeds.
S.PHON: == kawajna == *** tan…ôŇč miazińß atu mukan. sikapańßaj a saŇč…ôk i r~…ĺabii. u masaj-kir~…ĺaŇč a nipar~…ĺuma Ē~ °. mar~…ĺi ¶aŇčaj a bar~…ĺu tan…ôŇč mi ¶…ôbus tu akuti Ē~ °aj a nanum, tan…ôŇč mukan. kagak kakuniza subar~…ĺ :*** …ôukarjota sar~…ĺa…ôdan :*** pr~…ĺanta…ô panan :***** magnor~…ĺiopńßjta upiz :****** magnor~…ĺiopsida mata :**** dipsa ¶ar~…ĺ…ôs sapamat :*** ¶aprifor~…ĺia ¶…ôa…ô mikitinaj :*** r~…ĺoni ¶…ôra ńßi ¶ańßi ¶aaj :** r~…ĺ. japoni ¶a u siwkaj nu nipar~…ĺuma u san…ôk nu nipar~…ĺuma mar~…ĺańß…ô ¶i tu im…ôr~…ĺaŇč pasubana i ¶a ¶udadan a r~…ĺa ¶ur~…ĺ u zuma a ¶id…ôkaj a kamu, sauŇčaj misapaju Ē~ ° a ¶id…ôkaj u sur~…ĺit nu zuma a kamu-ńßur~…ĺam atu amir~…ĺika ** , . r~…ĺoni ¶…ôra japoni ¶a, known as gor~…ĺd…ôn-and-sir~…ĺ*…ôr ńßon…ôsu ¶kr~…ĺ…ô or *apan…ôs…ô ńßon…ôsu ¶kr~…ĺ…ô in …ôŇčr~…ĺisńß, suikazura in *apan…ôs…ô, jinjinńßua or r…ôndoŇčt…ôŇč in ¶ńßin…ôs…ô, indoŇčd…ôoŇčgur~…ĺ in kor…ôan, and kim Ňč*n ńßoa in *i…ôtnam…ôs…ô, is a sp…ô ¶i…ôs of ńßon…ôsu ¶kr~…ĺ…ô nati*…ô to …ôast…ôrn asia in ¶r~…ĺudiŇč ¶ńßina, *apan and kor…ôa. it is a twiniŇč *in…ô abr~…ĺ…ô to ¶r~…ĺimb up to ** m (** ft) ńßigńß or mor…ô in tr…ô…ôs, witńß opposit…ô, simpr~…ĺ…ô o*ar~…ĺ r~…ĺ…ôa*…ôs *** ¶m (*.***.* in) r~…ĺoŇč and *** ¶m (*.****.** in) broad. tńß…ô fr~…ĺow…ôrs ar…ô doubr~…ĺ…ô-toŇču…ôd, op…ôniŇč wńßit…ô and fadiŇč to j…ôr~…ĺr~…ĺow, and sw…ô…ôtr~…ĺj *anir~…ĺr~…ĺa s ¶…ônt…ôd. tńß…ô fruit is a br~…ĺa ¶k spńß…ôri ¶ar~…ĺ b…ôrrj *** mm (*.****.** in) diam…ôt…ôr ¶ontainiŇč a f…ôw s…ô…ôds.
S.FORM: == kawayna == ťáĎťäÄŤäĪ taneng miazih atu mukan. sikapahay a sangek i labii. u masay-kilang a nipaluma'. malicangay a balu taneng micebus tu akuti'ay a nanum, taneng mukan. kagak kakuniza subal :Áúüś†łŚüü Eukaryota salaedan :ś§ćÁČ©ÁēĆ Plantae panan :ŤĘęŚ≠źś§ćÁČ©ťĖÄ Magnoliophyta upiz :ťõôŚ≠źŤĎČś§ćÁČ©Á∂Ī Magnoliopsida mata :Ś∑ĚÁļĆśĖ∑Áõģ Dipsacales sapamat :ŚŅćŚÜ¨ÁßĎ Caprifoliaceae mikitinay :ŚŅćŚÜ¨ŚĪ¨ Lonicera hicahicaay :ŚŅćŚÜ¨ L. japonica u siwkay nu nipaluma u sanek nu nipaluma malaheci tu imelang pasubana i cacudadan a lacul u zuma a cidekay a kamu, saungay misapayu' a cidekay u sulit nu zuma a kamu-Hulam atu Amilika ŚŅćŚÜ¨ , . Lonicera japonica, known as golden-and-silver honeysuckle or Japanese honeysuckle in English, suikazura in Japanese, jinyinhua or rendongteng in Chinese, indongdeonggul in Korean, and kim ng√Ęn hoa in Vietnamese, is a species of honeysuckle native to eastern Asia including China, Japan and Korea. It is a twining vine able to climb up to 10 m (33 ft) high or more in trees, with opposite, simple oval leaves 3‚Äď8 cm (1.2‚Äď3.1 in) long and 2‚Äď3 cm (0.79‚Äď1.18 in) broad. The flowers are double-tongued, opening white and fading to yellow, and sweetly vanilla scented. The fruit is a black spherical berry 3‚Äď4 mm (0.12‚Äď0.16 in) diameter containing a few seeds.
